title: T-078 — UX implementation red-lines: factory_spec, BOM SSOT, RM usability, PO/TO and optional D365
prompt:
# T-078 — Apply final Technical UX red-lines to existing prototypes

When implementing any 03-TECHNICAL UI task, apply UX §0A red-lines on top of existing prototype parity. Preserve structural, visual and interaction parity with the referenced prototype files, but change production copy/guards to FG/WIP naming, Technical-owned factory_spec, shared BOM SSOT, clone-on-edit approval banner, RM usability panel, supplier_specs Phase 1 review language, PO/TO non-conformance trigger language, Quality-owned lab read-only labels, and optional D365 disabled states. This governance task does not build a screen by itself; it adds required acceptance criteria to all downstream UI closeouts.

pipeline_inputs.details: Use this as a UI governance task for ACP agents. Existing prototype labels may contain FA/PR or D365-first assumptions; agents must preserve layout and interactions while correcting domain copy and blocking states per PRD v3.3. For BOM detail, prototype path `design/Monopilot Design System/technical/bom-detail.jsx:650-930` is the visual anchor; for modals, use `design/Monopilot Design System/technical/modals.jsx:94-180` and `design/Monopilot Design System/technical/modals.jsx:371-475` where relevant. T-083 provides the local production copy/guard red-line overlay and must be treated as the concrete checklist for UI closeout copy assertions.
acceptance_criteria: Given a UI implementation references `design/Monopilot Design System/technical/bom-detail.jsx:650-930`, then it preserves structural, visual and interaction parity while showing FG/WIP naming and the active-edit creates new draft approval banner.

Given a BOM component modal references `design/Monopilot Design System/technical/modals.jsx:133-170`, then it preserves structural, visual and interaction parity while adding the RM usability check rows.
Given D365 UI references `design/Monopilot Design System/technical/modals.jsx:475-540`, then it preserves structural, visual and interaction parity and includes a disabled optional-integration state.
Given any Supplier Specs or Lab Results tab is implemented, then copy states supplier_specs are Technical Phase 1 master data and lab status is Quality-owned read-only.
Given T-083 red-line overlay exists, then UI task closeout references it and proves legacy copy/D365-first wording was not carried from prototypes into production UI.
test_strategy: For each UI task closeout, require screenshot/DOM assertions for legacy prototype parity plus domain red-line assertions.
Run the UI task-specific vitest/playwright command named in that task.
risk_red_lines: Do not rewrite prototype files for visual churn.
Do not keep FA/PR in production copy except when showing legacy imported source values.
Do not make this governance task a standalone screen implementation.
pipeline_inputs.description: UI governance red-lines applied to Technical prototype-derived implementation tasks.
